meta.name: Soul Food Potato Salad (Classic Creamy)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A classic Soul Food-style potato salad: creamy, tangy, lightly sweet, with eggs, celery, and relish. Built for cookouts and make-ahead serving, with clear potato cooking cues so the salad holds texture without turning gummy.

ingredients.label: Potatoes (Yukon Gold or russet)
ingredients.notes: About 3 lb. Yukon holds shape; russet is softer/creamier.

ingredients.label: Salt (for boiling water)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp, plus more to taste.

ingredients.label: Large eggs, hard-boiled
ingredients.notes: 5 chopped into salad; 1 reserved for garnish (optional).

ingredients.label: Mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Add more as needed for creaminess.

ingredients.label: Onion, finely diced
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Use yellow onion or sweet onion.

ingredients.label: Sugar (optional, if you want it slightly sweeter)

steps.title: Prep potatoes
steps.body: Peel potatoes (optional; many Soul Food versions are peeled) and cut into 2.5 cm (1-inch) chunks for even cooking. Rinse briefly to remove surface starch.

steps.title: Boil potatoes (texture control)
steps.body: Place potatoes in a large pot and cover with cold water by ~2.5 cm (1 inch). Salt the water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer. Cook 10–15 minutes until a fork slides in easily but pieces are not falling apart.

steps.title: Drain and steam-dry (prevents watery salad)
steps.body: Drain potatoes well. Return to the warm pot off-heat for 1–2 minutes to steam-dry, shaking gently. This helps dressing cling and keeps salad from getting watery.

steps.title: Cool slightly
steps.body: Let potatoes cool 10–15 minutes until warm (not hot). Warm potatoes absorb flavor; too hot can break down the mayo.

steps.title: Make dressing
steps.body: In a large bowl, mix mayonnaise, yellow mustard, optional Dijon, relish, paprika, pepper, and a portion of salt. Add optional pickle juice/vinegar/hot sauce if using.

steps.title: Add eggs and vegetables
steps.body: Chop 5 hard-boiled eggs and add to dressing along with celery, onion, and optional green onions. Mix to combine.

steps.title: Fold in potatoes (keep chunks)
steps.body: Add warm potatoes to bowl and fold gently until coated. If you like it creamier, mash a small portion of potatoes against the bowl and fold back in (optional).

steps.title: Taste and adjust
steps.body: Adjust salt, pepper, mustard, relish, and optional sugar to balance tang/sweet. Add a spoonful more mayo if needed for creaminess.

steps.title: Chill (make-ahead logic)
steps.body: Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours (best: overnight). Flavor improves as it rests.

steps.title: Garnish and serve
steps.body: Before serving, top with paprika and sliced reserved egg (optional). Serve cold or cool.

notes: Potato texture logic: cut evenly, simmer (not hard boil), and stop cooking when fork-tender but intact.
Steam-drying is a key step: it prevents watery potato salad and improves dressing cling.
Make-ahead: best after chilling 4–12 hours; refresh with a spoon of mayo if it tightens in the fridge.
Food safety: keep below 40°F (4°C). Do not leave out more than 2 hours (1 hour if very hot outdoors).
